name: veritas-default
description: Conservative starter policy template for a newly bootstrapped AI-guided repository.

rules.message: The tracked Veritas operational artifacts must stay present so agents and reviewers share the same baseline.

explain.summary: Keep the repo-local Veritas governance baseline present.
mustDo: Restore missing .veritas operational files before finishing.
mustNotDo: Do not delete or weaken governance files to make a run pass.
explain.exampleGood: Update the schema and default policy template together.

rules.message: The shipped Veritas CLI entrypoints and package manifest must stay present.

explain.summary: Keep the CLI and package entrypoints available.
mustDo: Preserve package metadata and shipped bin files.
mustNotDo: Do not move CLI entrypoints without updating package.json.
explain.exampleGood: Add a new subcommand in bin/veritas.mjs and export its runner.
explain.exampleBad: Delete bin/veritas-report.mjs while leaving it in package.json.

rules.message: The repo must keep the docs that explain the shipped Veritas surface.

explain.summary: Keep reference docs aligned with product core behavior.
mustDo: Update docs when schemas, commands, or artifact shapes change.
mustNotDo: Do not document behavior that is not wired.
explain.exampleGood: Document a new rule kind in artifacts-and-schemas.md.
explain.exampleBad: Claim a command exists before bin/veritas.mjs dispatches it.

rules.message: The shipped Veritas schema set must stay present and reviewable.

explain.summary: Schemas are the contract for repo-maps, repo standards, evidence, and standards-feedback.
mustDo: Update the relevant schema with any new persisted field.
mustNotDo: Do not add artifact fields that schemas reject.
explain.exampleGood: Add a rule kind to schema and evaluator registry together.
explain.exampleBad: Allow additionalProperties for unknown rule match shapes.

rules.message: All required AI tool instruction files must contain the Veritas governance block.

explain.summary: AI instruction files must carry the canonical governance block.
mustDo: Regenerate or reapply the governance block after edits.
mustNotDo: Do not hand-edit the marker-bounded block into a different meaning.
explain.exampleGood: Run veritas apply governance-blocks after changing instructions.
explain.exampleBad: Remove the governance block from CLAUDE.md.

rules.message: Prefer keeping the tracked Veritas evidenceCheck and repo conformance integration surfaces present so changes flow through the documented operational path before review.

explain.summary: Keep hook and repo conformance integration artifacts in place.
mustDo: Preserve the local hook and workflow paths or update this rule deliberately.
mustNotDo: Do not bypass repo conformance integration by deleting hook artifacts.
explain.exampleGood: Update both hook builder and default policy when adding a hook.

rules.message: Source files should not leave console.log diagnostics behind.

explain.summary: Remove ad hoc console.log calls from source files.
mustDo: Use structured reporting or tests instead of committed console.log calls.
mustNotDo: Do not leave temporary debugging output in src/**.
explain.exampleGood: Return explicit feedback from a CLI command.

rules.message: First-contact Veritas docs should use canonical product vocabulary from CONTEXT.md.

explain.summary: Keep public Veritas docs aligned with the repo glossary.
mustDo: Use CONTEXT.md terms in first-contact docs and guides.
mustNotDo: Do not reintroduce pre-glossary names for product concepts.
explain.exampleGood: Use Standards Feedback when describing observed improvement evidence.
explain.exampleBad: Use eval as the product term for standards improvement evidence.

terms.prefer: Readiness Report, Repo Conformance, or Standards Feedback

rules.message: Repeatable repo governance checks should be represented by Veritas primitives before they become local helper scripts.

explain.summary: Represent repeatable governance through Repo Standards Requirements, Repo Map Evidence Checks, Change Guidance, Readiness Reports, Repo Conformance, Standards Feedback, Standards Recommendations, Generated Evidence, or Protected Standards.
mustDo: Route governance-enforcing scripts through a Repo Map Evidence Check or express the behavior as a Repo Standards Requirement with actionable Change Guidance.
mustNotDo: Do not add repeatable governance checks that bypass Veritas primitives or hide policy behavior in an untracked helper script.
explain.exampleGood: Add an Evidence Check in .veritas/repo-map.json for a validation command, or add a Repo Standards Requirement that reports the policy result.
explain.exampleBad: Add an npm script that enforces repo governance without a matching Evidence Check or Requirement.

helperExemptions.name: prepublishOnly
helperExemptions.rationale: Packaging guard that composes routed verification before publish.

helperExemptions.name: test:coverage:check
helperExemptions.rationale: Coverage threshold helper used by routed verification.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:check
helperExemptions.rationale: Developer shortcut for a scoped Readiness Report.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:evidence-check
helperExemptions.rationale: Alias for the routed required evidence check.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:redact:check
helperExemptions.rationale: Local hygiene helper; not a Repo Map Evidence Check yet.

helperExemptions.name: lint:governance
helperExemptions.rationale: Feedback-format readiness shortcut for local review.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:readiness
helperExemptions.rationale: CLI alias that invokes Veritas readiness.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:conformance:report
helperExemptions.rationale: Generated Evidence command for repo-local conformance.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:conformance:readiness
helperExemptions.rationale: Generated Evidence command for readiness conformance.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:conformance
helperExemptions.rationale: Summarizes Generated Evidence; not a policy source.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:conformance:examples
helperExemptions.rationale: Builds Generated Evidence examples for documentation.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:conformance:verify
helperExemptions.rationale: Composes routed checks and generated conformance helpers.

helperExemptions.name: veritas:ci:conformance
helperExemptions.rationale: CI alias for conformance verification composition.
